search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae wrist sprain telehealth guideline PDF
site:nice.org.uk wrist hand pain scaphoid recommendations
site:doh.gov.ae hand pain guideline Abu Dhabi
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
The 2024 AAOS update replaced the 2016 edition.
Teaching material is not an exact guideline for this workflow.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for wrist sprain or strain and adult CTS contexts; not exact for every hand, finger, thumb, tendon, inflammatory, traumatic, or paediatric cause.
setting_applicability: Partial: Dubai telehealth, US orthopaedic guidance, and UK fracture services versus a broad ClinicNote workflow.
UAE_applicability: Direct for DHA sprain or strain documentation in Dubai, United Arab Emirates; CTS and fracture content require UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2, the 2024 AAOS CTS CPG, and current NICE NG38 were opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: DHA Issue 2, AAOS 2024 CTS, and NICE NG38 are current official versions identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed to limit DHA applicability to sprain and strain presentations; no broad hand-diagnosis items were mapped from scope alone.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines sprains and strains, common ankle, knee, wrist and muscle contexts, Dubai telehealth scope, injury mechanisms, athletic activity, overexertion, repetitive motion, and recurrence risks.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped wrist-sprain history, symptom, function, and examination fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports pain, swelling, bruising, restricted movement, weight-bearing difficulty, instability, loss of function, mechanism, timing, force, direction, pop or snap, aggravating conditions, and previous episodes.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped deformity, swelling, color, fever, movement, and neurovascular concern fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Identifies severe pain or swelling, discoloration, weakness, movement loss, numbness, coldness, deformity, persistent symptoms, fracture or dislocation concern, and clinician-selected radiography, ultrasound, or MRI.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-documented activity, bracing, analgesia, physiotherapy, and follow-up fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented RICE, activity protection, mobilization, analgesia, physical therapy, return to activity, one-week review, worsening review, and urgent escalation for severe, neurovascular, deformity, weight-bearing, fever, or hot-joint concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed to confirm the adult carpal-tunnel guideline population and boundaries; no legacy items were mapped from scope alone.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines the current adult carpal-tunnel diagnostic and management population and confirms the 2024 guideline scope.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult carpal-tunnel terminology and symptom-and-sign diagnostic context without inferring a diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports carpal-tunnel diagnostic terminology and clinician use of symptom-and-sign history within CTS-6, with selected ultrasound or electrodiagnostic testing when needed.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed for non-complex fracture assessment context; no wrist-specific rule components were mapped because the legacy fields were broader than the exact recommendation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.

unresolved_source_gaps: Broad hand, finger, thumb, tenosynovitis, nocturnal symptom, open-wound, bilateral numbness, Tinel, Phalen, Finkelstein, snuffbox, and fixed two-week follow-up fields remain unsupported.
The combined carpal-tunnel or strain diagnosis label remains clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
